summary.text: Provides extension methods for adding JavaScript applications to an

members.name: AddJavaScriptApp
members.signature: public static IResourceBuilder<JavaScriptAppResource> JavaScriptHostingExtensions.AddJavaScriptApp(this IDistributedApplicationBuilder builder, string name, string appDirectory, string runScriptName = "dev")

summary.text: Adds a JavaScript application resource to the distributed application using the specified app directory and run script.

remarks.text: If a Dockerfile does not exist in the application's directory, one will be generated automatically when publishing. The method configures the resource with Node.js defaults and sets up npm integration.

returns.text: A resource builder for the newly added JavaScript application resource.

appDirectory.text: The path to the directory containing the JavaScript application.

builder.text: The distributed application builder to which the JavaScript application resource will be added.

name.text: The unique name of the JavaScript application resource. Cannot be null or empty.

runScriptName.text: The name of the npm script to run when starting the application. Defaults to "dev". Cannot be null or empty.

members.name: AddNodeApp
members.signature: public static IResourceBuilder<NodeAppResource> JavaScriptHostingExtensions.AddNodeApp(this IDistributedApplicationBuilder builder, string name, string appDirectory, string scriptPath)

summary.text: Adds a node application to the application model. Node should be available on the PATH.

remarks.text: This method executes a Node script directly using
node script.js
. If you want to use a package manager you can add one and configure the install and run scripts using the provided extension methods. If the application directory contains a
package.json
file, npm will be added as the default package manager.

appDirectory.text: The path to the directory containing the node application.

scriptPath.text: The path to the script relative to the app directory to run.

description.text: Add a Node app to the application model using yarn and 'yarn run dev' for running during development:

members.name: AddViteApp
members.signature: public static IResourceBuilder<ViteAppResource> JavaScriptHostingExtensions.AddViteApp(this IDistributedApplicationBuilder builder, string name, string appDirectory, string runScriptName = "dev")

summary.text: Adds a Vite app to the distributed application builder.

remarks.text: The following example creates a Vite app using npm as the package manager.

appDirectory.text: The path to the directory containing the Vite app.

name.text: The name of the Vite app.

runScriptName.text: The name of the script that runs the Vite app. Defaults to "dev".

members.name: WithBrowserDebugger
members.signature: public static IResourceBuilder<T> JavaScriptHostingExtensions.WithBrowserDebugger<T>(this IResourceBuilder<T> builder, string browser = "msedge")

arguments.Description: Configures a browser debugger for the JavaScript application

summary.text: Configures a browser debugger for the JavaScript application resource, enabling browser-based debugging through a child resource that launches when the parent application is ready.

remarks.text: This method creates a child

that waits for the parent JavaScript application to start, then launches a browser debug session targeting the parent's HTTP or HTTPS endpoint. The parent resource must have at least one HTTP or HTTPS endpoint configured.

browser.text: The browser to use for debugging. Defaults to

builder.text: The resource builder for the JavaScript application.

T.text: The type of the JavaScript application resource.

description.text: Thrown when the parent resource does not have an HTTP or HTTPS endpoint, or when the IDE extension does not support browser debugging.
Add browser debugging to a JavaScript application:

members.name: WithBuildScript
members.signature: public static IResourceBuilder<TResource> JavaScriptHostingExtensions.WithBuildScript<TResource>(this IResourceBuilder<TResource> resource, string scriptName, string[]? args = null)

arguments.Description: Specifies an npm script to run before starting the application

summary.text: Adds a build script annotation to the resource builder using the specified command-line arguments.

remarks.text: Use this method to specify custom build scripts for JavaScript application resources during deployment.

returns.text: The same resource builder instance with the build script annotation applied.

args.text: An array of command-line arguments to use for the build script.

resource.text: The resource builder to which the build script annotation will be added.

scriptName.text: The name of the script to be executed when the resource is built.

TResource.text: The type of JavaScript application resource being configured.

members.name: WithBun
members.signature: public static IResourceBuilder<TResource> JavaScriptHostingExtensions.WithBun<TResource>(this IResourceBuilder<TResource> resource, bool install = true, string[]? installArgs = null)

arguments.Description: Configures Bun as the package manager

summary.text: Configures the JavaScript resource to use Bun as the package manager and optionally installs packages before the application starts.

remarks.text: Bun forwards script arguments without requiring the
command separator, so this method configures the resource to omit it. When publishing and a bun lockfile (
bun.lock
or
bun.lockb
) is present,
--frozen-lockfile

is used by default. Publishing to a container requires Bun to be present in the build image. This method configures a Bun build image when one is not already specified. To use a specific Bun version, configure a custom build image (for example,
oven/bun:<tag>
) using

install.text: When true (default), automatically installs packages before the application starts. When false, only sets the package manager annotation without creating an installer resource.

installArgs.text: Additional command-line arguments passed to "bun install". When null, defaults are applied based on publish mode and lockfile presence.

description.text: Run a Vite app using Bun as the package manager:

members.name: WithNpm
members.signature: public static IResourceBuilder<TResource> JavaScriptHostingExtensions.WithNpm<TResource>(this IResourceBuilder<TResource> resource, bool install = true, string? installCommand = null, string[]? installArgs = null)

arguments.Description: Configures npm as the package manager

summary.text: Configures the Node.js resource to use npm as the package manager and optionally installs packages before the application starts.

install.text: When true (default), automatically installs packages before the application starts. When false, only sets the package manager annotation without creating an installer resource.

installArgs.text: The command-line arguments passed to npm to install dependencies.

installCommand.text: The install command itself passed to npm to install dependencies.

members.name: WithPnpm
members.signature: public static IResourceBuilder<TResource> JavaScriptHostingExtensions.WithPnpm<TResource>(this IResourceBuilder<TResource> resource, bool install = true, string[]? installArgs = null)

arguments.Description: Configures pnpm as the package manager

summary.text: Configures the Node.js resource to use pnpm as the package manager and optionally installs packages before the application starts.

install.text: When true (default), automatically installs packages before the application starts. When false, only sets the package manager annotation without creating an installer resource.

installArgs.text: The command-line arguments passed to "pnpm install".

members.name: WithRunScript
members.signature: public static IResourceBuilder<TResource> JavaScriptHostingExtensions.WithRunScript<TResource>(this IResourceBuilder<TResource> resource, string scriptName, string[]? args = null)

arguments.Description: Specifies an npm script to run during development

summary.text: Adds a run script annotation to the specified JavaScript application resource builder, specifying the script to execute and its arguments during run mode.

remarks.text: Use this method to specify a custom script and its arguments that should be executed when the resource is executed in RunMode.

returns.text: The same resource builder instance with the run script annotation applied, enabling further configuration.

args.text: An array of arguments to pass to the script.

resource.text: The resource builder to which the run script annotation will be added.

scriptName.text: The name of the script to be executed when the resource is run.

TResource.text: The type of the JavaScript application resource being configured. Must inherit from JavaScriptAppResource.

members.name: WithViteConfig
members.signature: public static IResourceBuilder<ViteAppResource> JavaScriptHostingExtensions.WithViteConfig(this IResourceBuilder<ViteAppResource> builder, string configPath)

arguments.Description: Configures a custom Vite configuration file

summary.text: Configures the Vite app to use the specified Vite configuration file instead of the default resolution behavior.

remarks.text: Use this method to specify a specific Vite configuration file if you need to override the default Vite configuration resolution behavior.

configPath.text: The path to the Vite configuration file. Relative to the Vite service project root.

description.text: Use a custom Vite configuration file:
